title: Rocket Lab CEO Says Demand Is 'Super Healthy' As Defense, Neutron Backlog Builds
text: Rocket Lab executives highlighted accelerating launch demand, expanding defense contracts, and progress on the Neutron rocket as key momentum drivers following a record first quarter. The company's backlog surged to over $2 billion in contracted revenue, a 20% quarterly increase and 108% year-over-year growth. During Q1 2026, Rocket Lab signed 31 Electron and HASTE launch contracts plus five Neutron contracts, representing more launches booked in three months than during all of 2025. CEO Peter Beck stated that demand is just not one of the things to worry about, emphasizing the backlog remains super healthy for a number of years. The company recently secured a $190 million, 20-launch HASTE order supporting Department of Defense hypersonic testing capabilities. On the Neutron program, management indicated the medium-lift rocket remains on track for its inaugural launch later in 2026, citing progress on tank refinements, engine testing, and landing barge development. For the second quarter, Rocket Lab projected revenue between $225 million and $240 million, with adjusted EBITDA losses of $20 million to $26 million.
